mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary environment, reducing the cost of capital and supporting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite a low FEDFUNDS, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 7.5% indicates significant economic slack, prompting central bank intervention or fiscal stimulus, which typically supports equity markets as investors anticipate recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 7.5%, SPX 60d forward returns are predominantly negative or flat, failing to show a statistically significant positive trend (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales (RSAFS) growth combined with a very low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) suggests consumer resilience despite accommodative policy, indicating a strong demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% MoM and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% do not correlate with positive S&P 500 returns, or show a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) during a period of low interest rates signals a weakening supply side without monetary tightening, which can lead to concerns about future growth and negatively impact S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: economic slowdown with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive or flat when INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% MoM during periods of FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, contradicting the expected negative impact.

mechanism_narrative: A low level of housing starts (HOUST) below 900.0 Thousands indicates weak construction activity and broader economic demand weakness, which typically weighs on corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: economic contraction or housing market downturn
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 900.0, SPX 5d forward returns are consistently positive, or show no statistically significant negative correlation (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity, which underpins corporate revenues and generally supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term (20 trading days).
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are negative or flat during periods of consistent PAYEMS growth, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) annual growth above 3.0% indicates increasing inflation, which can erode corporate profit margins and lead to expectations of monetary tightening, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive or flat during periods of CPIAUCSL annual growth above 3.0%, or the expected negative correlation is absent (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) with annual growth above 2.0% signifies a robust and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of GDPC1 annual growth above 2.0% do not consistently lead to positive S&P 500 60d returns, or show a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.
